subdomains.meaning: DNS record exists but no public web service

soc_monitoring.reason: DNS entry exists without public service exposure

port_scan.note: Port scan includes open, closed, and filtered states. Filtered ports usually indicate firewall/CDN protection.

vulnerabilities.evidence: Response length changed compared to baseline
HTTP exposed; Missing X-Content-Type-Options; Missing Content-Security-Policy; Missing HSTS header; Missing X-Content-Type-Options; Missing Content-Security-Policy
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS and disable port 80 if not required.

vulnerabilities.evidence: HTTPS exposed; Missing X-Content-Type-Options; Missing Content-Security-Policy; Missing HSTS header; Missing X-Content-Type-Options; Missing Content-Security-Policy
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement strong security headers such as CSP, HSTS, and X-Frame-Options.

risk_summary.risk_context: Trusted organization with moderate security gaps

attack_path: Security header bypass and client-side attacks

attack_explanation.attacker_view: Exploitation may be possible depending on attacker skill and defenses
reasoning: Service on port 443 has high exposure, increasing attack likelihood.
Public web service allows endpoint enumeration and input-based vulnerability testing.
Service on port 80 has high exposure, increasing attack likelihood.
